notes: These sparse packs back time-resolved executable validation checks where a route model can be compared against published concentration snapshots at more than one time coordinate.
They are screening-oriented anchors tied to specific reviewed datasets, not full kinetic calibration targets.

points.title: Residual-air reentry start concentration vs chlorpyrifos residential broadcast study
points.note: Observed reentry-start room-air concentration at 4 hours post application is compared against the reported 61-94 ug/m3 chlorpyrifos band from the residential broadcast study, converted to mg/m3.

points.title: Residual-air room concentration at 24 hours vs chlorpyrifos residential broadcast study
points.note: Observed room-air concentration at 24 hours post application is compared against the approximately 30 ug/m3 chlorpyrifos level reported in the residential broadcast study, widened to a +/-10% screening band for comparability.

packs.note: Sparse two-point room-air series derived from the chlorpyrifos residential broadcast study, anchored at about 4 hours and 24 hours post application.

points.title: Residual-air room concentration at 24 hours vs diazinon office study
points.note: Observed room-air concentration at 24 hours post application is compared against the approximately 125 ug/m3 diazinon empty-office concentration reported one day after application, widened to a +/-10% screening band.

points.title: Residual-air room concentration at 48 hours vs diazinon office study
points.note: Observed room-air concentration at 48 hours post application is compared against the approximately 53 ug/m3 highest diazinon office concentration reported two days after application, widened to a +/-10% screening band.

packs.note: Sparse two-point office room-air series derived from the diazinon office post-application study, using the one-day and two-day high-air persistence observations as a screening anchor.

points.title: Air-space insecticide aerosol room concentration at 0.75 hours vs household mosquito aerosol study
points.note: Observed end-of-window room concentration at 0.75 hours is compared against the reported 0.0138 ppm prallethrin aerosol level at 30 to 45 minutes after use, converted to a narrow 0.14-0.20 mg/m3 screening band.

points.title: Air-space insecticide aerosol room concentration at 6 hours vs household mosquito aerosol study
points.note: Observed end-of-window room concentration at 6 hours is compared against the study statement that residues fell below 0.0001 ppm prallethrin by 6 hours, converted to an approximate <=0.0013 mg/m3 screening upper bound.

packs.note: Sparse two-point room-air decay series for household mosquito aerosol use, anchored at the reported 30 to 45 minute prallethrin concentration and the reported below-detection condition by 6 hours.
